title: Calopsis hyalina
taxonomy: Calopsis hyalina is a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae, commonly known as the geophytes or asphodels. It is native to the southern region of Africa. Calopsis hyalina is further classified under the subfamily Asphodeloideae. The genus Calopsis is a part of the larger group of plants that also includes the genera Asphodelus and Kniphofia. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Asphodelaceae family.

intro: Calopsis hyalina, also known as the golden trumpet or yellow trumpet, is a plant species in the family Aizoaceae, native to the arid and semi-arid regions of Southern Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Calopsis hyalina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Calopsis hyalina is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or extract, and is also used in the form of ointments and creams for topical application.
